sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient is a 36-year-old female who underwent bilateral reduction mammoplasty on 10/10/2020. She reports intermittent back pain, but states it is not currently present. She denies chills, fever, nausea, or vomiting. She describes enjoying summertime and reports no new family concerns. She uses scar gel twice daily on the scars, finding them bothersome. The patient has not felt the lump she previously identified by her previous physician, Ruth Sanchez, but continues to use scar gel.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The breasts appear well-healed with no signs of infection or redness. The incisions are well-healed. Examination reveals widened scars in the surgical field. Vital signs are within normal limits.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The primary concern is the persistent scar tissue discomfort. The patient's history of a palpable lump, now absent, warrants continued monitoring. The widened scars require attention.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Schedule a follow-up appointment in six months to reassess the scars. Continue using scar gel twice daily on the affected areas. Arrange photographs to document the scar progression. Advise the patient to contact the clinic if the discomfort worsens or does not improve.
